steps.title: Use the data directly
steps.content: Use the exponential form $A(t)=a(b)^t$. The initial balance gives $a=36{,}100$. The 13-year balance gives $$68{,}071.93=36{,}100(b)^{13}.$$

steps.title: Find the growth multiplier
steps.content:
Divide by $36{,}100$ and take the 13th root:
$$b=\left(\frac{68{,}071.93}{36{,}100}\right)^{1/13}\approx1.05.$$
So the model is $$A(t)=36100(1.05)^t.$$

steps.title: Compare with the choices
steps.content: $A \left(t\right)=36,100.00{ \left(1.05\right)}^{t}$ has both the correct starting value and the growth multiplier. The remaining alternative uses $0.05$, which would make the balance almost zero after 13 years. $A \left(t\right)=36,100.00{ \left(1.05\right)}^{t}$ is correct.
